title: Yank & Paste
intro: `y` yanks (copies) with any motion: `yy` a whole line, `yw` a word. `p` pastes after the cursor, `P` before it. Deleted text is also yanked — `dd` + `p` moves a line.

exercises.instruction: Classic typo fix: the letters are swapped. Swap them back with `xp` (delete the character, paste it after the next one).

hints: `yy` yanks the whole line; `p` pastes it below.

exercises.instruction: Move the first line below the second using `dd` then `p`.
hints: `dd` deletes (and yanks) the line; `p` pastes it below the cursor line.

exercises.instruction: Duplicate the word `hello` using `yw` then `P` (paste before the cursor).
hints: `yw` yanks the word plus its trailing space; `P` pastes it at the cursor.

exercises.instruction: Put these calls in order: delete the `second()` line with `dd`, then paste it below with `p`.
hints: `dd` yanks what it deletes — `p` drops it below the current line.

exercises.instruction: Make three copies of this line: yank it with `yy`, then press `p` twice.
hints: The yanked line stays in the register — every `p` pastes another copy.

exercises.instruction: Fix the typo `adn` with `xp` — the cursor is already on the `d`.
hints: `x` deletes the `d`, `p` pastes it after the `n`.
